implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-002 no Mode A pre-feature-spec stages dispatched
AC-003 no degraded-specialist rationale anywhere on chronology
AC-004 build pauses at pull-request-review with MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL gate
AC-005 PR URL recorded against the build
AC-006 /feature-plan dispatch context contains /feature-spec artefact paths
AC-007 autobuild forward context contains /feature-plan artefact paths
AC-008 ordering invariants (no autobuild before plan, no plan before spec)
AC-009 autobuild dispatched via dispatch_autobuild_async; wave/task indices visible
AC-010 supervisor remains responsive to status queries while autobuild in flight
AC-011 forge skip refused at PR-review with constitutional rationale; honoured at feature-plan
AC-012 forge cancel resolves as synthetic reject; build reaches CANCELLED
AC-013 test suite completes well under 30 seconds (0.07s observed)

completion_promises.criterion_text: Stage history contains exactly four entries in order: feature-spec, feature-plan, autobuild, pull-request-review (Group G data-integrity scenario)
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeBChainShape.test_chain_has_exactly_four_canonical_stages_in_order asserts that the chronology recorded by the supervisor's turn_recorder matches MODE_B_CANONICAL_ORDER == (FEATURE_SPEC, FEATURE_PLAN, AUTOBUILD, PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW) exactly.

completion_promises.criterion_text: No product-owner, architect, system-arch, or system-design entry appears (Group L positive assertion of no-PO/no-architect axiom)
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeBChainShape.test_no_product_owner_or_architect_or_system_stage_dispatched asserts that the chronology stages and the subprocess dispatcher's call list never intersect MODE_B_FORBIDDEN_HISTORY_STAGES (PRODUCT_OWNER/ARCHITECT/SYSTEM_ARCH/SYSTEM_DESIGN). The harness also wires _unreachable_specialist_dispatcher which raises AssertionError if any specialist dispatch is attempted under Mode B.

completion_promises.criterion_text: No degraded-specialist rationale appears anywhere on the build's stage history (Group L)
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeBChainShape.test_no_degraded_specialist_rationale_in_history walks every chronology row's rationale and asserts the substring 'degraded' is absent. TestModeBChainShape.test_reasoning_model_is_never_consulted_in_mode_b also confirms the reasoning model port (which would emit degraded-mode rationales) records zero calls.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Build pauses at pull-request-review with a MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL gate (Group A constitutional pin)
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeBPullRequestPause.test_pauses_at_pull_request_review asserts the final TurnReport is DISPATCHED with chosen_stage == PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW. test_records_mandatory_human_gate_mode_and_pr_url asserts the chronology row carries gate_mode == 'MANDATORY_HUMAN_APPROVAL'. test_pr_review_gate_received_auto_approve_false confirms the gate submission was made with auto_approve=False (operator approves per ASSUM-011).

completion_promises.criterion_text: PR URL is recorded against the build (Group H smoke acceptance)
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeBPullRequestPause.test_records_mandatory_human_gate_mode_and_pr_url asserts the terminal chronology row carries a non-empty pr_url that begins with 'https://github.com/example/forge/pull/'. The FakePRReviewGate mints monotonically increasing PR numbers so the URL is unique per submission.

completion_promises.criterion_text: /feature-plan dispatch context contains the /feature-spec artefact paths from the approved spec entry
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeBForwardPropagation.test_feature_plan_dispatch_carries_feature_spec_artefact_paths drives the smoke and inspects the forward_context list captured by FakeSubprocessDispatcher on the FEATURE_PLAN call. The dispatcher consults a real ForwardContextBuilder configured against the same stage_log + worktree allowlist the supervisor uses, so the assertion exercises the production MODE_B_PROPAGATION_CONTRACT row (FEATURE_PLAN <- FEATURE_SPEC, kind='path').

completion_promises.criterion_text: autobuild dispatch context contains the /feature-plan artefact paths from the approved plan entry
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeBForwardPropagation.test_autobuild_dispatch_context_carries_feature_plan_artefact_paths drives the smoke to PR pause (so FEATURE_PLAN is approved on the stage_log) and consults the same ForwardContextBuilder with stage=AUTOBUILD, mode=BuildMode.MODE_B. The resulting ContextEntry list is asserted to contain every PLAN_ARTEFACT_PATHS entry.

completion_promises.criterion_text: No autobuild dispatch was recorded before plan approval; no plan dispatch before spec approval (Group A 'supplied as input')
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeBForwardPropagation.test_autobuild_did_not_dispatch_before_plan_was_approved asserts FEATURE_PLAN's chronology index precedes AUTOBUILD's. test_feature_plan_did_not_dispatch_before_spec_was_approved asserts FEATURE_SPEC's chronology index precedes FEATURE_PLAN's. The supervisor's StageOrderingGuard belt-and-braces re-check (via MODE_B_PREREQUISITES) provides the actual enforcement; these assertions verify the runtime invariant.

completion_promises.criterion_text: autobuild is dispatched via dispatch_autobuild_async and the async_tasks state channel exposes wave/task indices during the run
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeBAsyncDispatch.test_autobuild_dispatched_via_autobuild_async asserts the FakeAutobuildAsyncDispatcher (sync, mirroring the production dispatch_autobuild_async shape) was invoked exactly once with the smoke build/feature, and that the FakeSubprocessDispatcher was NOT used for AUTOBUILD. test_async_tasks_channel_exposes_wave_and_task_indices drives until AUTOBUILD dispatch fires, snapshots the async_task_reader's state list, and asserts the entry carries feature_id == FEAT-B1, wave_index == 1, task_index == 1.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Supervisor remains responsive to status queries while autobuild is in flight (Group A async key-example)
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeBAsyncDispatch.test_supervisor_remains_responsive_to_status_query_in_flight primes the build through FEATURE_SPEC + FEATURE_PLAN, injects a sibling FEAT-OTHER-SIBLING autobuild as 'running_wave' on the async_tasks channel, and calls supervisor.next_turn. The call returns synchronously with TurnOutcome.WAITING_PRIOR_AUTOBUILD and zero autobuild dispatcher invocations — proving the supervisor refuses without blocking on a real subprocess.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A forge skip against the pull-request-review pause is refused with constitutional rationale (Group C); a forge skip against the feature-plan flag-for-review pause is honoured and the chain resumes (Group D edge-case)
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeBCliSteering.test_skip_against_pull_request_review_is_refused_constitutionally invokes a real CliSteeringHandler with handle_skip(stage=PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW) and asserts SkipStatus.REFUSED_CONSTITUTIONAL, presence of 'ADR-ARCH-026' in the rationale, no resume nudge, and the canonical CONSTITUTIONAL_STAGES set still contains PULL_REQUEST_REVIEW. test_skip_against_feature_plan_flag_for_review_is_honoured asserts handle_skip(stage=FEATURE_PLAN) returns SkipStatus.SKIPPED, the skip recorder fired, and the resume nudge was sent for the right (build_id, stage).

completion_promises.criterion_text: A forge cancel while paused at any pre-PR checkpoint resolves as a synthetic reject; build reaches cancelled terminal state
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeBCliSteering.test_cancel_during_pre_pr_pause_resolves_as_synthetic_reject configures the snapshot reader to report PAUSED_AT_GATE on FEATURE_PLAN (a pre-PR checkpoint), invokes handle_cancel, and asserts CancelStatus.CANCELLED_VIA_PAUSE_REJECT, that pause_reject_resolver was called with the right scope, that build_canceller.mark_cancelled fired (the build reaches CANCELLED), and that the rationale cites FEAT-FORGE-004 ASSUM-005.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Test runs in under 30 seconds with all dispatchers stubbed
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeBSmokeBudget.test_smoke_completes_within_thirty_seconds wraps a full drive_until_paused() call in time.monotonic() and asserts elapsed < 30.0. The full 19-test suite runs in 0.07s on the implementation host, well within budget.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Test file follows project Python conventions (PEP 8, snake_case for functions, PascalCase for classes, UPPER_CASE for constants), uses `from __future__ import annotations`, type hints throughout, dataclasses for value-shape doubles, pathlib-free (no path I/O needed), no bare except, no print statements. Module passes Python compilation and pytest collection without warnings. The project does not declare a strict lint configuration in pyproject.toml; the file matches the style established by the Mode A smoke (test_mode_a_smoke.py) and the supervisor mode-dispatch tests.
